title: James K. Polk and Manifest Destiny
description: How familiar are you with the events of the Mexican-American War and the westward march of Manifest Destiny?
topic_path: James K. Polk and Manifest Destiny

assessment_items.question_content:
The United States annexed Texas in 1845, but that didn’t end conflict with Mexico over the region; what dispute over Texas was a leading cause of the Mexican-American War?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Santa Anna did not send a telegram to Polk.

choices.content: Mexico claimed the southern border of Texas to be at the Nueces River, but the United States claimed that Texas’s border was further south, at the Rio Grande River.

The people of the Texas territory desired to join the United States as a slave state, but were denied; in retaliation, Texas declared itself part of Mexico, and the United States declared war to reclaim it.
Antonio Lopez de Santa Anna sent a threatening letter to President Polk; now known as the Santa Anna Telegram, the letter so insulted Polk that he committed troops to the Mexico-Texas border.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the United States acquire the land that became the states of California, New Mexico, Arizona, Nevada, Utah, and Colorado, and establish undisputed borders for Texas?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Louisiana Purchase took place about 40 years before the United States acquired the territories listed.

choices.content: These were concessions won in the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo.
Thomas Jefferson’s administration bought the territory from France in the Louisiana Purchase.
The Oregon Treaty of 1846 granted those lands to the United States from Britain.

assessment_items.question_content:
Who said **"Remember the Alamo!"** and what did this famous phrase mean? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: While the Alamo was a Catholic mission, the motivations for the attack were not religious.

choices.content: Sam Houston, the leader of the Tejano forces. He used this as a battle cry after Santa Anna and his troops attacked the Alamo.
Santa Anna, after he led the Mexican attack at the Battle of the Alamo.
President John Tyler, who used this phrase to warn against annexing Texas.

assessment_items.question_content:
What does the slogan “54-40 or fight!” refer to?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Polk did not propose that 40% of Americans would own property.

choices.content: James K. Polk’s assertion that the United States had the rights to the Oregon Territory, extending all the way up to Russian Alaska
The assertion that through conquest, the United States would reduce the number of states in Mexico from 54 to 40
It refers to the proposal that by the year 1854, 40% of Americans would own property.

assessment_items.question_content:
**James K. Polk**, the 11th U.S. President, oversaw a war with Mexico and expanded Oregon borders by negotiating with the British during his time in office. These actions associate him with the concept of

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Polk’s expansion is associated with an American sense of entitlement to settle US land.

choices.content: Manifest Destiny.
The Divine Right of Kings.
The State of Nature.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the **Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo** establish?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Puerto Rico did not become a US territory until 1898.

choices.content: The current border between the United States and Mexico
The status of Puerto Rico as a territory of the United States
The Good Neighbor Policy of preferential treatment for South American allies

assessment_items.question_content:
The term **“Manifest Destiny”** refers to white Americans’ belief that

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The term “Manifest Destiny” is not associated with the afterlife.

choices.content: they had the divine right to settle North America.
they deserved to live in integrated harmony with people of other cultures.
there were a list of goods owed to them in the afterlife, like inventory on a ship’s manifest.
